nodes.name: Math Theory 03 — Stochastic Calculus
nodes.description: Theory background note 3: stochastic calculus toolkit organized as the chain Brownian motion -> Ito integral -> SDE -> Fokker-Planck -> OU process, used to justify Layer S SDE system (S1)-(S3) and Theorems 4-5 for thermal noise, 1/f noise, and device variability.
nodes.author: ws

nodes.name: Brownian Motion (Wiener Process)
nodes.description: Stochastic process B(t) with B(0)=0, independent increments, and B(t)-B(s) ~ N(0, t-s). Nowhere differentiable with quadratic variation [B,B]_t = t. Models thermal white noise; dB^x, dB^W, dB^delta in Layer S SDEs (S1)-(S3).
nodes.author: ws

nodes.name: Ito Integral
nodes.description: Integral int f(t) dB(t) defined as L^2 limit of left-endpoint Riemann sums over adapted integrands, satisfying the Ito isometry E[(int f dB)^2] = E[int f^2 dt]. Non-anticipating (Ito) convention preserves martingale property, unlike Stratonovich.
nodes.author: ws

nodes.name: Ito's Lemma (Stochastic Chain Rule)
nodes.description: For dX = mu dt + sigma dB and f in C^{1,2}: df = (df/dt) dt + (df/dx) dX + (1/2)(d^2 f/dx^2) sigma^2 dt. The extra curvature term from (dB)^2 = dt is the source of noise-induced bias (1/2 Tr(sigma^2 H_V)) in Theorem 4 Lyapunov analysis.
nodes.author: ws

nodes.name: Stochastic Differential Equation (SDE) Framework
nodes.description: dX = mu(t,X) dt + sigma(t,X) dB with strong existence/uniqueness under Lipschitz + linear growth (Ito 1951). Layer S (S3) dW = (1/tau_W)(-W + eta S deltaW) dt + sigma_W dB^W is an instance; Layer D is the sigma=0 special case.
nodes.author: ws

nodes.name: Fokker-Planck Equation
nodes.description: PDE for the density p(t,x) of X(t): dp/dt = -d/dx(mu p) + (1/2) d^2/dx^2 (sigma^2 p), a.k.a. Kolmogorov forward equation. Derived by applying Ito's lemma to test functions and integrating by parts. Stationary solution (dp/dt = 0) gives long-run distribution (Gaussian for OU).
nodes.author: ws

nodes.name: Ornstein-Uhlenbeck Process
nodes.description: Linear-drift, constant-diffusion SDE dX = -theta(X - mu) dt + sigma dB. Mean-reverting with explicit solution; stationary distribution N(mu, sigma^2/(2 theta)). Canonical model for thermally fluctuating bias-clamped node; basis for Layer S Theorem 5 noise-tolerance bound.
nodes.author: ws

nodes.name: Martingale Convergence Theorem
nodes.description: For uniformly integrable martingale M(t): M(t) -> M_infty almost surely as t -> infty (Doob 1953). Since int f dB is a martingale (Ito integral with adapted f), combining with eta_t -> 0 kills stochastic part a.s. in Theorem 4 Step 3, leaving only drift (gradient flow).
nodes.author: ws

nodes.name: Lyapunov Equation (High-Dim OU Stationary Covariance)
nodes.description: For high-dimensional OU dX = -A X dt + Sigma_B dB (A positive definite), stationary covariance Sigma satisfies A Sigma + Sigma A^T = Sigma_B Sigma_B^T. Directly used in Theorem 5 proof step 2 to obtain Sigma = (sigma^2/(2 eta)) H^{-1}.
nodes.author: ws

nodes.name: Stochastic Calculus Connectivity Chain
nodes.description: Chain: Brownian motion -> Ito integral -> Ito lemma -> SDE -> {Fokker-Planck, OU/Lyapunov} -> Theorem 5 bound; Martingale convergence branches off to Theorem 4 almost-sure convergence. Captures why each tool is indispensable for Layer S's noise-tolerance statements.
nodes.author: ws
